anta: noun. A kind of pier that terminates the walls of the naos, a kind of post or pillar on either side of a doorway or entrance of a Greek

argon: noun. The chemical element (symbol Ar) with an atomic number of 18.
argot: noun. A secret language or conventional slang peculiar to thieves, tramps and vagabonds.
arrogant: adjective. Having excessive pride in oneself, often with contempt or disrespect for others.
art: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for artificial languages.
gan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gan.

groat: noun. Hulled grain, chiefly hulled oats.

oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
ora: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oroha.

roan: adjective. Having a coat of a dark base color with individual white hairs mixed in.

tag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tagoi.
tarn: noun. A small mountain lake, especially in Northern England.
taro: noun. Colocasia esculenta, raised as a food primarily for its corm, which distantly resembles potato.
tarragon: noun. A perennial herb, the wormwood species Artemisia dracunculus, from Europe and parts of Asia.
tog: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tonga (Malawi).
